Title: [Control Path: RNC] Runner Name: AzNetToolsRunners.RnmAsgUpdateAfterDeletingVMRunner Region: useast2euap Instance: azfRNCRnmAsgUpdateAfterDeletingVMRunneruseast2euapProd Phase: VM3_PING_VM1_Phase_2
RCATitle: wrongly pushed the config change before the SLB app is able to handle
RCADescription: Internal RCAWe introduced a new Service Tunnel Gateway VIP range (a /28 prefix) in the DCMT config file for the CBN (EastUS2EUAP) region to support the testing on ST migration for some banksy customer. In parallel, we made changes to the SLB application to exclude this new range for non-banksy customers.These two changes are in separate code bases. The DCMT configuration change was intended to be deployed only after the SLB App update was fully rolled out. However, the configuration change was checked in and rolled out before the corresponding SLB app change was rolled. As a result, SLB App was unable to handle the additional range mapping and began throwing exceptions. Consequently, the updates on CX's ST CA or normal CA (if it is in same bucket partition with ST CA) could not be processed by SLB, which led to customer VM connectivity impact. The issue was isolated to the CBN (EastUS2EUAP) region. MitigationWe mitigated the issue by rolling back the DCMT configuration to remove the newly added prefix. We then migrated the affected Vnets to ensure they are allocated with Service Tunnel addresses from the existing prefix. No further fixes are required. The problematic config change has been fully reverted in DCMT.
Mitigation: mitigated

RCATitle: [Control Path: NRP] Runner Name: AzNetToolsRunners.MgmtNic_VMSSWithEnicPnic Region: useast2euap Instance: azfRNCMgmtNic_VMSSWithEnicPnicuseast2euapProd Phase: DeleteRg
RCADescription: The reason is on compute side - enic was leaked and deletenic wasnt called for the rg.The new deployments did not wait for the cleanup of enics and did not call putnic for enic as well for two such deployments.On the third deployments, it triggered putnic for enic and triggered deletenic for enic and runner become healthy.On network side, the rg is created by runner infra and rg name is different each time. They do not know if on compute side if anything is leaked.On compute side, the custom code added uses same rg in each deployments and hence even if it leaked, it will go ahead with new deployments without cleaning the compute rg first.Repair item is created to delete existing rg if it already exist
Mitigation: Mitigating since the resources were deleted successfully and monitor has reported as healthy from last one hour

Title: [RunnerICM Aggregation CP] [Region: uscentraleuap] [Team Name: CLOUDNET\RNM]
RCATitle: New PubSubProxy feature caused a perf regions
RCADescription: The newly introduced feature of using PubSubProxy to publish to PubSub introduced lock contentions. The publishing logic in PubSubProxy is synchronous. Thus, it held the lock on name records longer than before when publishing was async when using PubSub client directly. A fix is to make the publishing async. New monitors will be created to cover this scenario and related.
Mitigation: NSMPlus regression. Turned off PbuSubProxy feature
